allies.rationale_text: Critical thinking is System 2 in operation — demanding evidence quality, testing logical validity, questioning assumptions, and withholding judgment until analysis is complete.
allies.source_quote: Critical thinking is System 2 in operation: demanding evidence quality, testing logical validity, questioning assumptions.

allies.rationale_text: Probabilistic thinking requires System 2 to compute distributions, reference classes, and confidence intervals — all explicitly deliberate operations that System 1 cannot perform.
allies.source_quote: Probabilistic thinking requires System 2 to compute distributions, reference classes, and confidence intervals.

allies.rationale_text: The pre-mortem is a deliberate System 2 technique that forces prospective scenario generation before commitment, directly fighting the WYSIATI closure that prevents System 1 from entertaining failure possibilities.
allies.source_quote: The pre-mortem is a deliberate System 2 technique: it forces prospective scenario generation before commitment.

allies.rationale_text: Deliberate practice converts System 2 operations into System 1 automaticity over time — System 2 is the scaffolding during skill acquisition, and deliberate practice is the mechanism of that conversion.
allies.source_quote: Deliberate practice converts System 2 operations into System 1 automaticity over time.

antagonists.rationale_text: System 1 suppresses System 2 engagement by delivering fluent, confident outputs that signal no further processing is needed — the smoother the System 1 answer, the less likely System 2 is to intervene.
antagonists.source_quote: System 1 suppresses System 2 engagement by delivering fluent, confident outputs that signal no further processing is needed.

antagonists.rationale_text: High cognitive load depletes the working memory and attentional resources that System 2 depends on — under pressure, time constraints, or information overload, System 2 capacity shrinks and System 1 assumes control.
antagonists.source_quote: High cognitive load depletes the working memory and attentional resources that System 2 depends on.

structured_tensions.tension_text: System-2 conflicts with cognitive-load-theory when high working memory demands deplete the attentional resources that deliberate System 2 reasoning requires, forcing default to System 1.
structured_tensions.source_quote: system-2 vs cognitive-load-theory: system-2 conflicts with cognitive-load-theory (cognitive load theory) when high working memory demands deplete the attentional resources that deliberate System 2 reasoning requires.

structured_tensions.tension_text: System-2 conflicts with commitment-bias when post-commitment ego investment redirects System 2 from auditing decisions to rationalizing them.
structured_tensions.source_quote: system-2 vs commitment-bias: system-2 conflicts with commitment-bias (commitment bias) when post-commitment ego investment redirects System 2 from auditing decisions to rationalizing them.

curation_notes.summary: System 2 Wave 3 curated from the new source markdown. Key allies: critical-thinking, probabilistic-thinking, premortem, deliberate-practice. Key antagonists: system-1 (suppresses engagement), cognitive-load-theory (depletes capacity). Key tensions: cognitive-load-theory, commitment-bias.
